heaviest_rainfall.status: On 09 November 2025, a record rainfall of 47.89mm was measured, indicating an exceptionally wet day.  24.6mm per hour was recorded as falling at times during the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain rate registered stands at 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, highlighting an extreme precipitation event.  21.19mm fell during the storm over 15.0 hours.

longest_rain.status: The record duration for continuous rain was set on 09 November 2025, the storm lasted 144.8 hours with a total precipitation of 47.89mm and it reached 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: Since records began on 11 April 2025, the highest lightning strike count in one day is 225 on 28 May 2026.

storm.status: There is a storm in the vicinity.  This is the first storm since 01 July 2026.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is no cold snap at the moment.  This year has experienced 3 cold snaps, with an average of 3.7 per year.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 27.7°C from 24 April 2026 (when the temperature reached 23.2°C but fell to -4.5°C).

heatwaves.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no heatwave in progress.  So far, 2 heatwaves have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was on 28 June 2026 and it has only happened 68 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (230°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 128.2 mph from 27 January 2026.

run.status: The highest wind run ever seen is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind speed recorded on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, a bit warmer for the feels like temperature (up 1.7°C).

The past week and month had observable variation in inside temperature (up 2.0°C) and cloud levels (down 252 m) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 497.8m higher than yesterday, 251.6m higher than the past week, 982.4m higher than the past month.

The cloud levels for yesterday ranged from 1083m to 4690m and averaged 3340m, while the past week and month indicated warmer conditions.
cloud levels.summary: The clouds will be high, plenty of sunshine is expected.

summary.summary: Inside; hot for most of the day. Outside; hot temperatures will prevail. Also, humidity will be quite oppressive and winds will be practically absent. Chance of light rain, the clouds will be high, plenty of sunshine is expected, and very bright daylight. No significant shifts in the weather are expected.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
